title: Child hospitalised after accident in York Street
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - A 12-year-old boy is recovering in George Hospital after he was hit by a Toyota Hilux-bakkie in York Street yesterday afternoon, Thursday 29 August, shortly after 14:00.


> Exact details surrounding the cause of the accident still have to be confirmed.


ER24 paramedics were on the scene swiftly. The child was stabilised and taken to hospital. He sustained a fractured humerus (upper arm) and scrape marks to his face and body.

Southern Cape police spokesperson Sergeant Chris Spies confirmed the accident and said George police opened a reckless and negligent driving docket following an incident.

"The cause of the incident is being investigated. The docket will be referred to the National Prosecuting Authority for a decision upon the conclusion of the police investigation," he said.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
